_description: Model derivations for Brazil vs Norway 2026-07-05 R16

rule_checks.RULE7: INACTIVE -- Norway win=20% is above the 15% threshold, genuinely competitive underdog not a blowout risk
rule_checks.RULE8: borderline FIRES -- Draw=25.81%, just over the 25% threshold
rule_checks.WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS_APPLIED: Q14 (Brazil 6+ SOT) is a DIRECT repeat of the Cluster-B trap: market-fit lambda (5.10, ->40.2%) sits below Brazil's own empirical SOT average (6.5, ->63.1%). Per the validated cross-match finding (0-for-5 net -155.64 RBP on exactly this move), staying at the market (0.41) rather than blending upward. This is a deliberate, disciplined non-repeat of a now well-established losing pattern.

espn_insight.brazil_blowout_coast_pattern: 0 second-half goals in either group win where they led -- do not inflate Brazil-scores-both-halves-type props (not directly asked here but informs 2nd-half-more-goals reasoning)
espn_insight.brazil_opponent_stats_not_suppressed: Haiti and Scotland both kept generating real stats (offsides, corners, SOT) even while being blown out 0-3 -- informs NOT suppressing Norway's own team-level numbers just because Brazil is favored
espn_insight.norway_regime_coverage: Norway's SOT was actually higher against a tougher opponent (Senegal, 7) than a weak one (Iraq, 5) -- genuine evidence they don't collapse against better sides, unlike Canada's untested-in-a-blowout floor
espn_insight.haaland_tested_in_knockout: Scored in all 3 appearances including the R32 knockout game vs Ivory Coast -- meaningfully better regime coverage than Ødegaard's assist streak (all 3 of his assists came against weaker opposition than Brazil)
